failureClusters.title: Weak citation grounding
failureClusters.evidence: Briefings include plausible strategic claims that are only loosely connected to packet excerpts.

failureClusters.title: Coverage misses governance risk
failureClusters.evidence: Outputs mention speed and cost but underweight human approval and holdout-case boundaries.

failureClusters.title: Cost tradeoff too vague
failureClusters.evidence: Recommendations do not quantify retry or trace costs even when source notes provide ratios.

featuredCase.title: Eval loop strategy briefing
featuredCase.sourceExcerpt: Coverage can improve while grounding regresses, so citation support needs an explicit shipping gate.
featuredCase.baseline: The team should expand automated evals because coverage improved and review time dropped.
featuredCase.candidate: The team should expand smaller eval batches only if citation support remains above the guardrail; coverage gains alone are not enough to ship.
featuredCase.evaluatorNote: Candidate preserves the positive eval-loop recommendation while grounding the shipping condition in the guardrail note.

recommendation.label: recommended
recommendation.text: Promote the citation-gated prompt variant to a larger visible eval run, while keeping holdout cases read-only until the run store is Zod-validated.
recommendation.warning: Cost is still inside guardrail, but the next target plan should replace these static artifacts with validated fixtures.
